text.title: vector-database-setup Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for vector-database-setup as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Storing full document text as metadata (kilobytes per record).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Metadata is loaded into memory at query time. Large payloads bloat RAM and slow query throughput. Store text externally; keep only IDs and small filter fields in metadata.

failure-mode.description: Using a cloud managed DB (Pinecone Serverless) for hard real-time SLAs without testing cold-start latency.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Serverless instances have cold-start latency on the first query after idle periods. Not suitable for sub-100ms P99 requirements.

failure-mode.description: Not implementing a document update/delete strategy before bulk ingestion.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Vector DBs have no referential integrity. Deleted source documents leave stale chunks in the index. Plan delete+re-insert or soft-delete tracking before the first ingest.

failure-mode.description: Agents calling delete_collection without human confirmation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: There is no undo. Collection deletion must always require human confirmation.

failure-mode.description: pgvector IVFFlat index without VACUUM ANALYZE after bulk inserts.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The query planner will not use the index without VACUUM ANALYZE; agents running bulk loads must include this step.
